title: Donanemab in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer Disease: The TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 Trial
abstract: Importance: Donanemab, an antibody that targets deposited amyloid-β, has demonstrated clearance of amyloid plaques and slowing of cognitive decline... Objective: To evaluate the efficacy and safety of donanemab in early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease... Design: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial... Results: Among 1736 participants, donanemab significantly slowed clinical decline...
conclusions: Donanemab significantly slowed clinical decline by 35% on the primary endpoint (iADRS)
47% of participants had complete amyloid clearance at 1 year
Serious adverse events occurred in 24% of participants, including 3 treatment-related deaths
Benefit was greater in participants with tau levels indicating early disease

key_findings.finding: Primary endpoint: 35% slowing of clinical decline (P < .001)
Complete amyloid clearance in 47% at 1 year

figure_2.label: Figure 2
figure_2.caption: Change from baseline in iADRS score over 76 weeks
